title: Global Markets Brief — 29 May 2026
intro: Risk appetite remains constructive in Asian trading as markets digest mixed overnight signals from Fed officials and ongoing geopolitical tensions in Eastern Europe. Japanese equities surge 2.58% following BoJ dovish rhetoric, while European indices close lower on energy sector weakness. USD remains bid against most majors, though NZD outperforms on RBNZ hawkish positioning expectations.

sections.heading: 🌍 Global Market Briefing — Asian Session
sections.content: Risk appetite remains constructive in Asian trading as markets digest mixed overnight signals from Fed officials and ongoing geopolitical tensions in Eastern Europe. Japanese equities surge 2.58% following BoJ dovish rhetoric, while European indices close lower on energy sector weakness. USD remains bid against most majors, though NZD outperforms on RBNZ hawkish positioning expectations.

sections.heading: 📊 FOREX — Currency Strength
sections.content:
1. NZD (+0.47%) - RBNZ hawkish bets drive rally
2. AUD (+0.04%) - Commodity strength supportive
3. USD (+0.04% avg) - Fed resilience maintains bid
4. CHF (+0.04%) - Safe haven flows muted
5. CAD (-0.01%) - Oil weakness weighs
6. GBP (-0.01%) - BoE uncertainty lingers
7. EUR (-0.09%) - ECB dovish pivot continues

sections.heading: 💱 Key Currency Pairs
sections.content:
EUR/USD 1.1644: Bearish bias intact below 1.1680 resistance. ECB dovish shift versus Fed hawkish hold maintains downward pressure. Target 1.1580 support.
USD/JPY 159.2930: Bullish momentum accelerating on BoJ intervention fears subsiding. Break above 160.00 psychological level opens path to 162.50.
NZD/USD 0.5961: Strong intraday performer on RBNZ rate hike speculation. Critical resistance at 0.6000; break targets 0.6080.

sections.heading: 💰 FX Flows & Positioning
sections.content:
• Real money EUR selling continues into month-end rebalancing
• Japanese pension funds reducing USD hedges on intervention concerns
• Commodity currency positioning turns constructive on supply constraints
• EM FX benefiting from reduced Fed tightening expectations

sections.heading: 📈 EQUITIES — Indices
sections.content: Nikkei 225 leads (+2.58% to 66,362) on BoJ dovish surprise and tech sector strength. US indices mixed overnight: Nasdaq +0.91% (26,917), S&P 500 +0.55% (4,754.60), Dow flat +0.05% (50,669). European weakness persists: FTSE -0.75%, DAX -0.34%, CAC -0.23% on energy sector rotation and growth concerns.

sections.heading: 🛢️ COMMODITIES
sections.content: Gold: $4,542.80 (+0.23%) - Consolidating near record highs; central bank buying supports. Silver: $76.12 (+0.27%) - Following gold's lead with industrial demand intact. WTI Crude: $87.79 (-1.25%) - Correction from technical overbought; $85.00 key support. Natural Gas: $3.299 (+0.43%) - Winter demand forecasts supportive.

sections.heading: ₿ CRYPTO
sections.content:
BTC: $73,476 (-0.07%) - Consolidating below $74,000 resistance; support at $72,000 holding firm. Institutional accumulation continues.
ETH: $2,008.41 (+0.07%) - Outperforming BTC marginally; ETH/BTC ratio stabilizing at 0.027. Staking yield compression weighs.
XRP: $1.3097 (-0.29%) - Regulatory overhang persists
SOL: $81.87 (-0.14%) - DeFi ecosystem growth supportive
DOGE: $0.0993 (-0.12%) - Retail interest waning

sections.heading: 📈 LONG
sections.content:
• USD/JPY - BoJ intervention fears receding, momentum strong
• NZD/USD - RBNZ hawkish pivot, commodity strength
• Nikkei 225 - BoJ accommodation, tech sector rally
• Gold - Central bank buying, inflation hedge demand
📉 SHORT
• EUR/USD - ECB dovish shift, growth concerns
• WTI Crude - Technical correction, demand concerns
• European banks - Rate cut expectations rising
• Bitcoin - Consolidation pattern, institutional profit-taking

sections.heading: 📅 Today's Macro Drivers
sections.content: German inflation data, US PCE deflator revision, RBNZ speakers, and China manufacturing PMI. Monitor 10-year yield dynamics around 4.25% and VIX below 15.
